title: MetaState: Persistent Working Memory for Discrete Diffusion Language Models
authors: Kejing Xia, Mingzhe Li, Lixuan Wei, Zhenbang Du, Xiangchi Yuan, Qirui Jin, Wenke Lee
abstract: Discrete diffusion language models (dLLMs) generate text by iteratively denoising a masked sequence. Compared with autoregressive models, this paradigm naturally supports parallel decoding, bidirectional context, and flexible generation patterns. However, standard dLLMs condition each denoising step only on the current hard-masked sequence, while intermediate continuous representations are discarded after sampling and remasking. We refer to this bottleneck as the \textbf{Information Island} problem. It leads to redundant recomputation across steps and can degrade cross-step consistency. We address this limitation with \textbf{MetaState}, a lightweight recurrent augmentation that equips a frozen dLLM backbone with a persistent, fixed-size working memory that remains independent of sequence length. \textbf{MetaState} consists of three trainable modules: a cross-attention Mixer that reads backbone activations into memory slots, a GRU-style Updater that integrates information across denoising steps, and a cross-attention Injector that feeds the updated memory back into backbone activations. We train these modules with $K$-step unrolling to expose them to multi-step denoising dynamics during fine-tuning. On LLaDA-8B and Dream-7B, \textbf{MetaState} introduces negligible trainable parameters while keeping the backbone frozen, and it consistently improves accuracy over frozen baselines. These results demonstrate that persistent cross-step memory is an effective mechanism for bridging denoising steps and improving generation quality in discrete diffusion language models.
title_zh: MetaState：离散扩散语言模型的持久工作记忆
abstract_zh: 离散扩散语言模型（dLLMs）通过迭代去噪被掩码的序列来生成文本。与自回归模型相比，这种范式天然支持并行解码、双向上下文建模以及灵活的生成模式。然而，标准的dLLMs在每个去噪步骤中仅基于当前的硬掩码序列进行条件生成，而中间的连续表示在采样和重新掩码后被丢弃。我们称这一瓶颈为**信息孤岛**（Information Island）问题。该问题导致各步骤间出现冗余的重复计算，并可能降低跨步骤的一致性。为解决这一局限，我们提出**MetaState**——一种轻量级的循环增强机制，为冻结的dLLM主干网络配备了一个持久的、固定大小的工作记忆，其大小与序列长度无关。**MetaState**包含三个可训练模块：一个交叉注意力混合器（Mixer），用于将主干网络的激活信息读入记忆槽；一个类似GRU的更新器（Updater），用于在去噪步骤间整合信息；以及一个交叉注意力注入器（Injector），将更新后的记忆反馈回主干网络的激活中。我们在微调过程中采用K步展开（K-step unrolling）训练这些模块，使其能够暴露于多步去噪动态中。在LLaDA-8B和Dream-7B上，**MetaState**引入的可训练参数极少，同时保持主干网络冻结，却能持续提升生成准确率，显著优于冻结基线模型。结果表明，持久的跨步骤记忆是一种有效机制，能够连接去噪步骤，显著提升离散扩散语言模型的生成质量。

title: MigMate: A VS Code Extension for LLM-based Library Migration of Python Projects
authors: Matthias Kebede, May Mahmoud, Mohayeminul Islam, Sarah Nadi
abstract: Modern software relies heavily on third-party software libraries to streamline the development process. The act of switching one library for a similar counterpart, called library migration, naturally occurs as libraries become outdated or unsuitable for the project. Manually migrating from one library to another is a time-consuming task. Our previous research developed MigrateLib, a command-line LLM-based migration tool that can automate the complete migration process. In this paper, we present our open-source VS Code IDE plugin, MigMate, that builds on MigrateLib by integrating the automated migration process into the developer's existing development environment. MigMate provides an interactive experience, allowing developers to view and confirm changes before they are applied. A preliminary user study shows that plugin usage consistently reduces the time taken to complete a library migration task, and it scores highly on the System Usability Scale.
title_zh: MigMate：基于大语言模型的 Python 项目库迁移 VS Code 扩展
abstract_zh: 现代软件开发高度依赖第三方软件库以加快开发进程。当现有库过时或不再适合项目需求时，用功能相似的替代库进行替换，即库迁移，便成为一种自然发生的行为。手动完成库迁移是一项耗时的任务。我们之前的研究开发了 MigrateLib，这是一个基于命令行的LLM迁移工具，能够自动化完成整个迁移过程。本文中，我们介绍了开源的VS Code IDE插件MigMate，该插件基于MigrateLib，将自动化迁移流程无缝集成到开发者的现有开发环境中。MigMate提供交互式体验，使开发者能够在应用更改前查看并确认变更内容。初步用户研究表明，使用该插件能持续减少完成库迁移任务所需的时间，并在系统可用性量表（System Usability Scale）上获得较高评分。

title: S5-HES Agent: Society 5.0-driven Agentic Framework to Democratize Smart Home Environment Simulation
authors: Akila Siriweera, Janani Rangila, Keitaro Naruse, Incheon Paik, Isuru Jayanada
abstract: The smart home is a key domain within the Society 5.0 vision for a human-centered society. Smart home technologies rapidly evolve, and research should diversify while remaining aligned with Society 5.0 objectives. Democratizing smart home research would engage a broader community of innovators beyond traditional limited experts. This shift necessitates inclusive simulation frameworks that support research across diverse fields in industry and academia. However, existing smart home simulators require significant technical expertise, offer limited adaptability, and lack automated evolution, thereby failing to meet the holistic needs of Society 5.0. These constraints impede researchers from efficiently conducting simulations and experiments for security, energy, health, climate, and socio-economic research. To address these challenges, this paper presents the Society 5.0-driven Smart Home Environment Simulator Agent (S5-HES Agent), an agentic simulation framework that transforms traditional smart home simulation through autonomous AI orchestration. The framework coordinates specialized agents through interchangeable large language models (LLMs), enabling natural-language-driven end-to-end smart home simulation configuration without programming expertise. A retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipeline with semantic, keyword, and hybrid search retrieves smart home knowledge. Comprehensive evaluation on S5-HES Agent demonstrates that the RAG pipeline achieves near-optimal retrieval fidelity, simulated device behaviour and threat scenarios align with real-world IoT datasets, and simulation engine scales predictably across home configurations, establishing a stable foundation for Society 5.0 smart home research. Source code is available under the MIT License at https://github.com/AsiriweLab/S5-HES-Agent.
title_zh: S5-HES Agent：面向社会5.0的智能代理框架，推动智能家居环境仿真的民主化
abstract_zh: 智能家居是“社会5.0”以人为本社会愿景中的关键领域。智能家居技术发展迅速，相关研究应不断多样化，同时保持与社会5.0目标的一致性。推动智能家居研究的民主化，将吸引更多来自产业界和学术界的非传统专家群体参与创新。这一转变需要具备包容性的仿真框架，以支持跨学科、跨领域的研究工作。然而，现有的智能家居仿真工具普遍需要较高的技术门槛，适应性有限，且缺乏自动化演进能力，难以满足社会5.0所倡导的全面需求。这些局限性严重阻碍了研究人员在安全、能源、健康、气候及社会经济等领域的高效仿真与实验。为应对上述挑战，本文提出了一种由社会5.0驱动的智能家居环境仿真代理（S5-HES Agent），这是一种基于智能体的仿真框架，通过自主的AI编排机制，实现了对传统智能家居仿真的革新。该框架利用可互换的大语言模型（LLMs）协调各类专业智能体，支持通过自然语言实现端到端的智能家居仿真配置，无需编程知识。系统采用融合检索增强生成（RAG）的管道，结合语义搜索、关键词搜索与混合搜索策略，高效获取智能家居知识。对S5-HES Agent的全面评估表明，RAG管道实现了接近最优的检索保真度，仿真设备行为与威胁场景与真实物联网数据集高度一致，且仿真引擎在不同家庭配置下表现出可预测的可扩展性，为社会5.0背景下的智能家居研究奠定了稳定基础。项目源代码已开源，遵循MIT许可证，可通过 https://github.com/AsiriweLab/S5-HES-Agent 获取。

title: GAM-RAG: Gain-Adaptive Memory for Evolving Retrieval in Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Yifan Wang, Mingxuan Jiang, Zhihao Sun, Yixin Cao, Yicun Liu, Keyang Chen, Guangnan Ye, Hongfeng Chai
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) grounds large language models with external evidence, but many implementations rely on pre-built indices that remain static after construction. Related queries therefore repeat similar multi-hop traversal, increasing latency and compute. Motivated by schema-based learning in cognitive neuroscience, we propose GAM-RAG, a training-free framework that accumulates retrieval experience from recurring or related queries and updates retrieval memory over time. GAM-RAG builds a lightweight, relation-free hierarchical index whose links capture potential co-occurrence rather than fixed semantic relations. During inference, successful retrieval episodes provide sentence-level feedback, updating sentence memories so evidence useful for similar reasoning types becomes easier to activate later. To balance stability and adaptability under noisy feedback, we introduce an uncertainty-aware, Kalman-inspired gain rule that jointly updates memory states and perplexity-based uncertainty estimates. It applies fast updates for reliable novel signals and conservative refinement for stable or noisy memories. We provide a theoretical analysis of the update dynamics, and empirically show that GAM-RAG improves average performance by 3.95% over the strongest baseline and by 8.19% with 5-turn memory, while reducing inference cost by 61%. Our code and datasets are available at: https://anonymous.4open.science/r/GAM_RAG-2EF6.
title_zh: GAM-RAG：用于检索增强生成中动态检索的增益自适应记忆
abstract_zh: 检索增强生成（RAG）通过引入外部证据来增强大型语言模型的能力，但许多现有实现依赖于构建后即固定的预建索引。因此，相关查询往往重复进行类似的多跳遍历，导致延迟增加和计算开销上升。受认知神经科学中基于模式的学习启发，我们提出了一种无需训练的框架GAM-RAG，该框架能够从重复或相关的查询中积累检索经验，并随时间动态更新检索记忆。GAM-RAG构建了一个轻量级、无关系依赖的分层索引，其链接捕捉的是潜在共现关系，而非固定的语义关联。在推理阶段，成功的检索经历会提供句子级别的反馈，更新句子记忆，使得未来面对类似推理任务时，相关证据更易被激活。为在噪声反馈下平衡记忆的稳定性与适应性，我们引入了一种基于不确定性的、受卡尔曼滤波启发的增益规则，该规则联合更新记忆状态与基于困惑度的不确定性估计。该机制对可靠的新信号采用快速更新，对稳定或噪声较大的记忆则进行保守优化。我们对更新动态进行了理论分析，并通过实验证明，GAM-RAG在最强基线基础上平均性能提升3.95%，在5轮记忆条件下提升达8.19%，同时将推理成本降低61%。相关代码与数据集已公开，地址为：https://anonymous.4open.science/r/GAM_RAG-2EF6。

title: Reasoning Core: A Scalable Procedural Data Generation Suite for Symbolic Pre-training and Post-Training
authors: Valentin Lacombe, Valentin Quesnel, Damien Sileo
abstract: Training on verifiable symbolic data is a promising way to expand the reasoning frontier of language models beyond what standard pre-training corpora provide. Yet existing procedural generators often rely on fixed puzzles or templates and do not deliver the distributional breadth needed at scale. We introduce Reasoning Core, a scalable suite that procedurally generates verifiable symbolic reasoning data across core formal domains: PDDL planning over randomized domains, first-order logic with equality, context-free grammar parsing and generation, causal reasoning over random Bayesian networks, and systems of equations. Each task is paired with an external solver for rigorous verification and admits continuous difficulty control for curriculum design. Examples can optionally include solver-derived reasoning traces, enabling supervised training from the earliest pre-training stages, and the same interface provides verifiable reward functions for reinforcement learning. Our experiments show that mixing Reasoning Core data into pre-training improves downstream reasoning while preserving, or slightly improving, language modeling quality. Zero-shot evaluations confirm these tasks challenge frontier models such as GPT-5. The code and data are publicly available under the MIT license.
title_zh: 推理核心：一种可扩展的程序化数据生成套件，用于符号预训练与后训练
abstract_zh: 在可验证的符号数据上进行训练，是拓展语言模型推理能力边界的一种有前景的方法，能够突破标准预训练语料库的限制。然而，现有的程序化生成器通常依赖于固定的谜题或模板，难以在大规模下提供所需的分布广度。我们提出了 Reasoning Core，这是一个可扩展的工具套件，能够跨核心形式化领域程序化生成可验证的符号推理数据，包括：在随机领域上的 PDDL 规划、带等式的一阶逻辑、上下文无关文法的解析与生成、基于随机贝叶斯网络的因果推理，以及方程组系统。每个任务都配备外部求解器以实现严格的验证，并支持连续的难度控制，便于课程设计。示例数据可选择性地包含由求解器生成的推理轨迹，从而支持从最初始的预训练阶段就进行监督训练；同一接口还为强化学习提供了可验证的奖励函数。实验表明，在预训练中引入 Reasoning Core 数据，能够提升下游推理性能，同时保持或略微提升语言建模质量。零样本评估结果证实，这些任务对 GPT-5 等前沿模型构成了实质性挑战。代码与数据已根据 MIT 许可证公开发布。

title: Tool Verification for Test-Time Reinforcement Learning
authors: Ruotong Liao, Nikolai Röhrich, Xiaohan Wang, Yuhui Zhang, Yasaman Samadzadeh, Volker Tresp, Serena Yeung-Levy
abstract: Test-time reinforcement learning (TTRL) has emerged as a promising paradigm for self-evolving large reasoning models (LRMs), enabling online adaptation on unlabeled test inputs via self-induced rewards through majority voting. However, a spurious yet high-frequency unverified consensus can become a biased and reinforced reward signal, leading to incorrect mode collapse. We address this failure mode with T^3RL (Tool-Verification for Test-Time Reinforcement Learning), which introduces test-time tool verification into reward estimation. Concretely, a verifier uses an external tool as evidence (e.g., from code execution) to upweight verified rollouts in a verification-aware voting, producing more reliable pseudo-labels for training. Across various math difficulties (MATH-500, AMC, and AIME 2024) and diverse backbone types, T^3RL significantly improves over TTRL, with larger gains on harder problems. More broadly, T^3RL can be viewed as verified online data synthesis, highlighting test-time tool verification as a key mechanism for stabilizing self-evolution.
title_zh: 测试时强化学习的工具验证
abstract_zh: 测试时强化学习（TTRL）作为一种有前景的范式，正推动大型推理模型（LRMs）的自我演化，使其能够通过多数投票生成的自诱导奖励，在未标注的测试输入上实现在线适应。然而，一种虚假但高频的未经验证的共识可能成为有偏且被强化的奖励信号，导致错误的模式坍缩。为此，我们提出了T^3RL（测试时工具验证），以解决这一失效模式。T^3RL在奖励估计中引入了测试时的工具验证机制。具体而言，验证器利用外部工具作为证据（例如代码执行结果），在验证感知的投票过程中对已验证的轨迹进行加权，从而生成更可靠的伪标签用于训练。在多种数学难度任务（MATH-500、AMC 和 AIME 2024）以及不同骨干模型架构上，T^3RL均显著优于TTRL，且在更难的问题上提升更为明显。更广泛地看，T^3RL可被视为一种经过验证的在线数据生成方法，凸显了测试时工具验证作为稳定自我演化过程的关键机制。

title: Recursive Models for Long-Horizon Reasoning
authors: Chenxiao Yang, Nathan Srebro, Zhiyuan Li
abstract: Modern language models reason within bounded context, an inherent constraint that poses a fundamental barrier to long-horizon reasoning. We identify recursion as a core principle for overcoming this barrier, and propose recursive models as a minimal realization, where the model can recursively invoke itself to solve subtasks in isolated contexts. We prove that any computable problem admits a recursive decomposition in which each subtask requires only exponentially smaller active context than standard autoregressive models; this strictly surpasses any context management approach confined to a single sequence, such as summarization. We further generalize our framework to modern agentic systems with arbitrary context processing and control flows, and prove that recursive models can achieve optimal power within this broader class. Experimentally, we train a 3B model to reason recursively and evaluate on Boolean satisfiability, a task requiring long-horizon combinatorial search, where it significantly outperforms frontier LLMs.
title_zh: 长周期推理的递归模型
abstract_zh: 现代语言模型在有限的上下文范围内进行推理，这一固有约束构成了长程推理的根本障碍。我们识别出递归是克服这一障碍的核心原则，并提出了递归模型作为最小化的实现方式：模型能够递归地自我调用，在隔离的上下文中解决子任务。我们证明，任何可计算的问题都可以被递归分解，其中每个子任务所需的活跃上下文规模仅为标准自回归模型的指数级缩小；这严格超越了任何局限于单一序列的上下文管理方法（如摘要）。我们进一步将该框架推广至具有任意上下文处理和控制流的现代智能体系统，并证明递归模型在此更广泛类别中可达到最优能力。实验上，我们训练了一个30亿参数的模型进行递归推理，并在布尔可满足性问题上进行评估，该任务需要长程组合搜索，结果表明其显著优于当前前沿的大语言模型。

title: Learning from Synthetic Data Improves Multi-hop Reasoning
authors: Anmol Kabra, Yilun Yin, Albert Gong, Kamilė Stankevičiūtė, Dongyoung Go, Johann Lee, Katie Z. Luo, Carla P. Gomes, Kilian Q. Weinberger
abstract: Reinforcement Learning (RL) has been shown to significantly boost reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs) in math, coding, and multi-hop reasoning tasks. However, RL fine-tuning requires abundant high-quality verifiable data, often sourced from human annotations, generated from frontier LLMs, or scored by LLM-based verifiers. All three have considerable limitations: human-annotated datasets are small and expensive to curate, LLM-generated data is hallucination-prone and costly, and LLM-based verifiers are inaccurate and slow. In this work, we investigate a cheaper alternative: RL fine-tuning on rule-generated synthetic data for multi-hop reasoning tasks. We discover that LLMs fine-tuned on synthetic data perform significantly better on popular real-world question-answering benchmarks, despite the synthetic data containing only fictional knowledge. On stratifying performance by question difficulty, we find that synthetic data teaches LLMs to compose knowledge -- a fundamental and generalizable reasoning skill. Our work highlights rule-generated synthetic reasoning data as a free and scalable resource to improve LLM reasoning capabilities.
title_zh: 从合成数据中学习可提升多跳推理能力
abstract_zh: 强化学习（RL）已被证明能显著提升大型语言模型（LLMs）在数学、编程以及多跳推理任务中的推理能力。然而，RL微调需要大量高质量且可验证的数据，这些数据通常来自人工标注、前沿大模型生成，或由基于LLM的验证器打分。这三种数据来源均存在显著局限：人工标注数据集规模小且成本高昂；LLM生成的数据容易产生幻觉且代价高；而基于LLM的验证器则准确率低且速度慢。在本研究中，我们探索了一种更经济的替代方案：使用规则生成的合成数据进行多跳推理任务的RL微调。我们发现，尽管合成数据仅包含虚构知识，但经过其微调的LLM在主流真实世界问答基准测试中表现显著优于基线模型。通过按题目难度分层分析性能，我们发现合成数据能够教会LLM进行知识组合——这是一种基础且可泛化的推理能力。本研究强调，规则生成的合成推理数据是一种免费且可扩展的资源，可用于有效提升LLM的推理能力。

title: Expanding LLM Agent Boundaries with Strategy-Guided Exploration
authors: Andrew Szot, Michael Kirchhof, Omar Attia, Alexander Toshev
abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) has demonstrated notable success in post-training large language models (LLMs) as agents for tasks such as computer use, tool calling, and coding. However, exploration remains a central challenge in RL for LLM agents, especially as they operate in language-action spaces with complex observations and sparse outcome rewards. In this work, we address exploration for LLM agents by leveraging the ability of LLMs to plan and reason in language about the environment to shift exploration from low-level actions to higher-level language strategies. We thus propose Strategy-Guided Exploration (SGE), which first generates a concise natural-language strategy that describes what to do to make progress toward the goal, and then generates environment actions conditioned on that strategy. By exploring in the space of strategies rather than the space of actions, SGE induces structured and diverse exploration that targets different environment outcomes. To increase strategy diversity during RL, SGE introduces mixed-temperature sampling, which explores diverse strategies in parallel, along with a strategy reflection process that grounds strategy generation on the outcomes of previous strategies in the environment. Across UI interaction, tool-calling, coding, and embodied agent environments, SGE consistently outperforms exploration-focused RL baselines, improving both learning efficiency and final performance. We show that SGE enables the agent to learn to solve tasks too difficult for the base model.
title_zh: 以策略引导探索拓展大语言模型智能体的边界
abstract_zh: 强化学习（RL）在大语言模型（LLM）的后训练阶段已展现出显著成效，使其作为代理在计算机操作、工具调用和编程等任务中表现出色。然而，探索（exploration）仍是LLM代理在强化学习中面临的核心挑战，尤其是在语言-动作空间中，环境观测复杂且奖励稀疏。本文提出一种新的探索方法——策略引导探索（Strategy-Guided Exploration, SGE），通过利用LLM在语言层面规划和推理环境的能力，将探索从低层次动作转移到高层次语言策略层面。SGE首先生成一个简洁的自然语言策略，描述为达成目标应采取的行动方向，然后基于该策略生成具体的环境动作。通过在策略空间而非动作空间中进行探索，SGE实现了结构化且多样化的探索，能够有效覆盖不同的环境结果。为在强化学习过程中提升策略多样性，SGE引入了混合温度采样机制，实现多种策略的并行探索；同时设计了策略反思机制，使策略生成能够基于先前策略在环境中产生的实际结果进行调整与优化。在用户界面交互、工具调用、编程以及具身代理等各类环境中，SGE均显著优于专注于探索的强化学习基线方法，不仅提升了学习效率，也显著改善了最终性能。实验表明，SGE使代理能够学会解决原始基础模型无法应对的复杂任务。

title: Production-Grade AI Coding System for Client-Side Development
authors: Ruihan Wang, Chencheng Guo, Guangjing Wang
abstract: Deploying large language model-based code generation in real-world client-side development remains challenging due to heterogeneous inputs, strict engineering constraints, and complex interaction logic expressed in product requirement documents (PRDs). Existing design-to-code approaches often focus on visual translation or single-shot generation, and struggle to reliably align generated code with production requirements. This paper presents a production-grade AI coding system designed for client-side development under realistic industrial constraints. The system adopts a structured, multi-stage pipeline that integrates Figma designs, natural-language PRDs, and domain-specific engineering knowledge into explicit intermediate artifacts, enabling controlled planning and incremental code generation. By grounding PRD understanding in concrete UI components, the system improves alignment between product requirements and implementation. We evaluate the system on proprietary but realistic datasets derived from production client-side projects. Results show that domain-specific adaptation significantly improves PRD understanding accuracy, while end-to-end evaluations demonstrate high UI fidelity and robust implementation of interaction logic in real-world cases. These findings suggest that structured, artifact-centric pipelines provide a practical foundation for production-grade AI coding systems.
title_zh: 适用于客户端开发的生产级人工智能编码系统
abstract_zh: 在真实世界中的客户端开发中部署基于大语言模型的代码生成仍面临诸多挑战，主要源于输入的异构性、严格的工程约束以及产品需求文档（PRD）中表达的复杂交互逻辑。现有的设计到代码（design-to-code）方法通常聚焦于视觉转换或单次生成，难以可靠地将生成的代码与生产环境需求对齐。本文提出了一种面向客户端开发、适用于真实工业环境约束的生产级AI编码系统。该系统采用结构化、多阶段的处理流程，将Figma设计稿、自然语言形式的PRD以及领域特定的工程知识整合为明确的中间产物，从而实现可控的规划与增量式代码生成。通过将PRD的理解建立在具体的UI组件之上，系统显著提升了产品需求与实际实现之间的对齐度。我们在源自真实生产客户端项目的专有但具有代表性的数据集上对系统进行了评估。结果表明，领域特定的适配显著提升了PRD理解的准确性，端到端评估也验证了系统在真实场景中具备高保真的UI还原能力以及稳健的交互逻辑实现。这些发现表明，结构化、以中间产物为中心的流水线为构建生产级AI编码系统提供了切实可行的基础。

title: Decoding Answers Before Chain-of-Thought: Evidence from Pre-CoT Probes and Activation Steering
authors: Kyle Cox, Darius Kianersi, Adrià Garriga-Alonso
abstract: As chain-of-thought (CoT) has become central to scaling reasoning capabilities in large language models (LLMs), it has also emerged as a promising tool for interpretability, suggesting the opportunity to understand model decisions through verbalized reasoning. However, the utility of CoT toward interpretability depends upon its faithfulness -- whether the model's stated reasoning reflects the underlying decision process. We provide mechanistic evidence that instruction-tuned models often determine their answer before generating CoT. Training linear probes on residual stream activations at the last token before CoT, we can predict the model's final answer with 0.9 AUC on most tasks. We find that these directions are not only predictive, but also causal: steering activations along the probe direction flips model answers in over 50% of cases, significantly exceeding orthogonal baselines. When steering induces incorrect answers, we observe two distinct failure modes: non-entailment (stating correct premises but drawing unsupported conclusions) and confabulation (fabricating false premises). While post-hoc reasoning may be instrumentally useful when the model has a correct pre-CoT belief, these failure modes suggest it can result in undesirable behaviors when reasoning from a false belief.
title_zh: 思维链前解码答案：来自预思维链探测与激活操控的证据
abstract_zh: 随着思维链（Chain-of-Thought, CoT）在大型语言模型（LLMs）中推理能力扩展方面变得日益核心，它也逐渐成为一种有前景的可解释性工具，暗示着可以通过模型的口头化推理过程来理解其决策机制。然而，CoT在可解释性方面的有效性取决于其忠实性——即模型所陈述的推理过程是否真实反映了其内在的决策过程。我们提供了机制层面的证据，表明指令微调后的模型通常在生成CoT之前就已经确定了最终答案。通过对CoT生成前最后一个token的残差流激活值训练线性探测器，我们能够在大多数任务上以0.9的AUC值预测模型的最终答案。我们发现，这些激活方向不仅具有预测能力，还具有因果性：沿着探测器方向调整激活值，会使模型答案发生改变的情况超过50%，显著高于正交基线。当这种调整导致错误答案时，我们观察到两种截然不同的失败模式：非蕴含（即陈述正确的前提，但得出无支持的结论）和虚构（即编造虚假的前提）。尽管在模型已有正确预推理信念的情况下，事后推理可能具有实用价值，但这些失败模式表明，当模型基于错误信念进行推理时，这种机制可能导致不可取的行为。

title: SciDER: Scientific Data-centric End-to-end Researcher
authors: Ke Lin, Yilin Lu, Shreyas Bhat, Xuehang Guo, Junier Oliva, Qingyun Wang
abstract: Automated scientific discovery with large language models is transforming the research lifecycle from ideation to experimentation, yet existing agents struggle to autonomously process raw data collected from scientific experiments. We introduce SciDER, a data-centric end-to-end system that automates the research lifecycle. Unlike traditional frameworks, our specialized agents collaboratively parse and analyze raw scientific data, generate hypotheses and experimental designs grounded in specific data characteristics, and write and execute corresponding code. Evaluation on three benchmarks shows SciDER excels in specialized data-driven scientific discovery and outperforms general-purpose agents and state-of-the-art models through its self-evolving memory and critic-led feedback loop. Distributed as a modular Python package, we also provide easy-to-use PyPI packages with a lightweight web interface to accelerate autonomous, data-driven research and aim to be accessible to all researchers and developers.
title_zh: SciDER：面向科学数据的端到端研究人员
abstract_zh: 基于大型语言模型的自动化科学发现正在重塑从构想到实验的科研全流程，然而现有智能体在自主处理科学实验所采集的原始数据方面仍面临挑战。我们提出了SciDER，一个以数据为中心的端到端自动化科研系统，全面覆盖科研生命周期。与传统框架不同，SciDER通过专用智能体协作，能够解析并分析原始科学数据，基于数据特征生成假设与实验设计，并自动编写与执行相应代码。在三个基准测试中的评估表明，SciDER在数据驱动的科学发现任务中表现卓越，其自演化记忆机制与基于批评反馈的闭环优化策略，使其显著优于通用型智能体及当前最先进的模型。SciDER以模块化Python包形式发布，同时提供轻量级Web界面和易于使用的PyPI包，旨在加速自主化、数据驱动的科研进程，并致力于让所有研究人员与开发者都能便捷使用。

title: Toward Graph-Tokenizing Large Language Models with Reconstructive Graph Instruction Tuning
authors: Zhongjian Zhang, Xiao Wang, Mengmei Zhang, Jiarui Tan, Chuan Shi
abstract: The remarkable success of large language models (LLMs) has motivated researchers to adapt them as universal predictors for various graph-related tasks, with the ultimate goal of developing a graph foundation model that generalizes diverse scenarios. The key challenge is to align graph data with language spaces so that LLMs can better comprehend graphs. As a popular paradigm, Graph-Tokenizing LLMs (GTokenLLMs) encode complex structures and lengthy texts into a graph token sequence, and then align them with text tokens via language instructions tuning. Despite their initial success, our information-theoretic analysis reveals that existing GTokenLLMs rely solely on text supervision from language instructions, which achieve only implicit graph-text alignment, resulting in a text-dominant bias that underutilizes graph context. To overcome this limitation, we first prove that the alignment objective is upper-bounded by the mutual information between the input graphs and their hidden representations in the LLM, which motivates us to improve this upper bound to achieve better alignment. To this end, we further propose a reconstructive graph instruction tuning pipeline, RGLM. Our key idea is to reconstruct the graph information from the LLM's graph token outputs, explicitly incorporating graph supervision to constrain the alignment process. Technically, we embody RGLM by exploring three distinct variants from two complementary perspectives: RGLM-Decoder from the input space; RGLM-Similarizer and RGLM-Denoiser from the latent space. Additionally, we theoretically analyze the alignment effectiveness of each variant. Extensive experiments on various benchmarks and task scenarios validate the effectiveness of the proposed RGLM, paving the way for new directions in GTokenLLMs' alignment research.
title_zh: 基于重构图指令微调的图标记化大语言模型
abstract_zh:
大型语言模型（LLMs）的显著成功激发了研究人员将其适配为各类图相关任务的通用预测器，其最终目标是构建一个能够泛化多种场景的图基础模型。核心挑战在于如何将图数据与语言空间对齐，从而使LLM能够更好地理解图结构。作为一种流行范式，图标记化语言模型（GTokenLLMs）将复杂的图结构和长文本编码为图标记序列，并通过语言指令微调将其与文本标记对齐。尽管取得了初步成功，但我们的信息论分析表明，现有GTokenLLMs仅依赖语言指令中的文本监督，仅实现了隐式的图-文对齐，导致文本主导偏差，未能充分挖掘图上下文信息。

为克服这一局限，我们首先证明了对齐目标的上界由输入图与其在LLM中隐藏表示之间的互信息所决定，这启发我们通过提升该上界来实现更优的对齐效果。为此，我们进一步提出了一种重构式图指令微调框架——RGLM。其核心思想是：从LLM输出的图标记中重构原始图信息，显式引入图监督以约束对齐过程。在技术实现上，我们从两个互补视角探索了RGLM的三种不同变体：RGLM-Decoder（从输入空间出发）、RGLM-Similarizer与RGLM-Denoiser（从隐空间出发）。此外，我们还对每种变体的对齐有效性进行了理论分析。

在多个基准数据集和任务场景下的大量实验验证了所提RGLM的有效性，为GTokenLLMs的对齐研究开辟了新的方向。

title: Generative AI in Software Testing: Current Trends and Future Directions
authors: Tanish Singla, Qusay H. Mahmoud
abstract: This paper investigates current software testing systems and explores how artificial intelligence, specifically Generative AI, can be integrated to enhance these systems. It begins by examining different types of AI systems and focuses on the potential of Generative AI to transform software testing processes by improving test coverage, increasing efficiency, and reducing costs. The study provides a com-prehensive overview of the current applications of AI in software testing, emphasizing its significant contributions in areas such as test case generation and validation. Through an extensive literature re-view, it highlights how Generative AI can streamline these processes, resulting in more robust and thorough testing outcomes. The paper also examines methods to improve the efficiency of Generative AI systems, such as prompt engineering and fine-tuning. Additionally, it explores the use of AI in specific tasks, including input generation, oracle generation, data generation, test data creation, and test case prioritization. By analyzing the current landscape and identifying both the opportunities and challenges in integrating Generative AI, this paper provides valuable insights and recommendations for practitioners and researchers. It underscores the need for ongoing advancements and targeted development efforts to overcome existing hurdles and fully leverage AI's capabilities. The findings further show that with continued innovation and careful implementation, Generative AI has the potential to significantly enhance the efficiency, effectiveness, and reliability of software testing, particularly in the rapidly evolving field of IoT testing.
title_zh: 生成式人工智能在软件测试中的应用：当前趋势与未来方向
abstract_zh: 本文研究了当前的软件测试系统，并探讨了如何将人工智能，特别是生成式人工智能（Generative AI），融入其中以提升测试系统的性能。文章首先分析了不同类型的人工智能系统，重点聚焦于生成式人工智能在提升测试覆盖率、提高测试效率以及降低测试成本方面所具备的巨大潜力，从而推动软件测试流程的变革。研究全面回顾了人工智能在软件测试中的当前应用，强调其在测试用例生成与验证等关键领域的重要贡献。通过广泛的文献综述，文章指出生成式人工智能能够有效优化这些流程，从而实现更稳健、更全面的测试结果。此外，本文还探讨了提升生成式人工智能系统效率的方法，如提示工程（prompt engineering）和模型微调（fine-tuning）。同时，文章深入分析了人工智能在具体任务中的应用，包括输入数据生成、预期结果（oracle）生成、数据生成、测试数据创建以及测试用例优先级排序等。通过对当前技术格局的分析，本文识别出集成生成式人工智能所面临的机遇与挑战，并为从业者与研究人员提供了宝贵的见解与建议。文章强调，必须持续推进技术进步并开展有针对性的研发工作，以克服现有障碍，充分释放人工智能的潜力。研究结果进一步表明，通过持续创新与审慎实施，生成式人工智能有望显著提升软件测试的效率、效果与可靠性，尤其在快速发展的物联网（IoT）测试领域具有广阔前景。

title: Recursive Think-Answer Process for LLMs and VLMs
authors: Byung-Kwan Lee, Youngchae Chee, Yong Man Ro
abstract: Think-Answer reasoners such as DeepSeek-R1 have made notable progress by leveraging interpretable internal reasoning. However, despite the frequent presence of self-reflective cues like "Oops!", they remain vulnerable to output errors during single-pass inference. To address this limitation, we propose an efficient Recursive Think-Answer Process (R-TAP) that enables models to engage in iterative reasoning cycles and generate more accurate answers, going beyond conventional single-pass approaches. Central to this approach is a confidence generator that evaluates the certainty of model responses and guides subsequent improvements. By incorporating two complementary rewards-Recursively Confidence Increase Reward and Final Answer Confidence Reward-we show that R-TAP-enhanced models consistently outperform conventional single-pass methods for both large language models (LLMs) and vision-language models (VLMs). Moreover, by analyzing the frequency of "Oops"-like expressions in model responses, we find that R-TAP-applied models exhibit significantly fewer self-reflective patterns, resulting in more stable and faster inference-time reasoning. We hope R-TAP pave the way evolving into efficient and elaborated methods to refine the reasoning processes of future AI.
title_zh: LLM和VLM的递归思维-回答过程
abstract_zh: 像 DeepSeek-R1 这类思考-作答推理模型通过利用可解释的内部推理过程取得了显著进展。然而，尽管模型频繁出现“Oops!”之类的自我反思提示，它们在单次推理过程中仍容易产生输出错误。为解决这一局限性，我们提出了一种高效的递归思考-作答过程（Recursive Think-Answer Process, R-TAP），使模型能够进行迭代式推理循环，从而生成更准确的答案，突破传统单次推理方法的限制。该方法的核心是一个置信度生成器，用于评估模型回答的确定性，并指导后续的改进过程。通过引入两种互补的奖励机制——递归置信度提升奖励与最终答案置信度奖励，我们证明，经过 R-TAP 增强的模型在大型语言模型（LLMs）和视觉-语言模型（VLMs）上均能持续优于传统的单次推理方法。此外，通过对模型输出中“Oops!”类表达的频率进行分析，我们发现应用 R-TAP 的模型表现出显著更少的自我反思模式，从而实现了更稳定、更快速的推理过程。我们希望 R-TAP 能为未来高效且精细的推理优化方法的发展铺平道路。

title: "When to Hand Off, When to Work Together": Expanding Human-Agent Co-Creative Collaboration through Concurrent Interaction
authors: Kihoon Son, Hyewon Lee, DaEun Choi, Yoonsu Kim, Tae Soo Kim, Yoonjoo Lee, John Joon Young Chung, HyunJoon Jung, Juho Kim
abstract: Human collaborators coordinate dynamically through process visibility and workspace awareness, yet AI agents typically either provide only final outputs or expose read-only execution processes (e.g., planning, reasoning) without interpreting concurrent user actions on shared artifacts. Building on mixed-initiative interaction principles, we explore whether agents can achieve collaborative context awareness -- interpreting concurrent user actions on shared artifacts and adapting in real-time. Study 1 (N=10 professional designers) revealed that process visibility enabled reasoning about agent actions but exposed conflicts when agents could not distinguish feedback from independent work. We developed CLEO, which interprets collaborative intent and adapts in real-time. Study 2 (N=10, two-day with stimulated recall interviews) analyzed 214 turns, identifying five action patterns, six triggers, and four enabling factors explaining when designers choose delegation (70.1%), direction (28.5%), or concurrent work (31.8%). We present a decision model with six interaction loops, design implications, and an annotated dataset.
title_zh: 何时交接，何时协作：通过并发互动拓展人机协同创作合作
abstract_zh: 人类合作者通过流程可见性和工作空间意识实现动态协作，而人工智能代理通常仅提供最终输出，或仅展示只读的执行过程（如规划、推理），无法解释用户对共享成果的并发操作。基于混合主动性交互原则，我们探讨了代理是否能够实现协作上下文感知——即理解用户对共享成果的并发操作并实时适应。研究1（N=10位专业设计师）表明，流程可见性有助于理解代理行为，但当代理无法区分用户反馈与独立工作时，会暴露冲突。为此，我们开发了CLEO系统，能够解读协作意图并实时调整。研究2（N=10，为期两天并结合刺激回忆访谈）分析了214轮交互，识别出五种行为模式、六种触发因素以及四种促成因素，解释了设计师在何种情境下选择委托（70.1%）、指导（28.5%）或并行工作（31.8%）。本文提出了一个包含六个交互循环的决策模型，总结了设计启示，并提供了一个带注释的数据集。

title: Surgical Post-Training: Cutting Errors, Keeping Knowledge
authors: Wenye Lin, Kai Han
abstract: Enhancing the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) via post-training is often constrained by the trade-off between efficiency and catastrophic forgetting. While prior research emphasizes the role of on-policy data in mitigating forgetting, we uncover--and validate both theoretically and empirically--an overlooked yet critical mechanism: the implicit regularization inherent in Direct Preference Optimization's (DPO) reward estimate. This motivates our Surgical Post-Training (SPoT), a new paradigm designed to optimize reasoning efficiently while preserving learned prior knowledge. SPoT consists of: (1) a data rectification pipeline that employs an Oracle to surgically correct erroneous steps via minimal edits, generating data proximal to the model's distribution; and (2) a reward-based binary cross-entropy objective. Unlike the relative ranking in DPO, this objective treats reasoning correctness as a binary classification problem, enforcing decoupled supervision signals. Empirically, with only 4k rectified math data pairs, SPoT improves Qwen3-8B's accuracy by 6.2% on average across in-domain and OOD tasks, requiring merely 28 minutes of training on 8x H800 GPUs. Code: https://github.com/Visual-AI/SPoT
title_zh: 外科术后训练：切割错误与知识保持
abstract_zh: 通过后训练提升大型语言模型（LLMs）的推理能力，通常受限于效率与灾难性遗忘之间的权衡。尽管以往研究强调了在线策略数据在缓解遗忘方面的作用，但我们通过理论分析与实证验证，揭示并证实了一个被忽视但至关重要的机制：直接偏好优化（DPO）中奖励估计所蕴含的隐式正则化。这一发现促使我们提出一种全新的范式——外科式后训练（Surgical Post-Training, SPoT），旨在高效优化推理能力的同时，有效保留模型已学习的先验知识。SPoT 包含两个核心组件：(1) 一种数据修正管道，利用“Oracle”对错误推理步骤进行精准修正，仅通过最小程度的编辑生成贴近模型分布的数据；(2) 基于奖励的二元交叉熵目标函数。与 DPO 中的相对排序不同，该目标将推理正确性视为二分类问题，从而施加解耦的监督信号。实验结果表明，仅使用 4,000 对经修正的数学数据，SPoT 在 Qwen3-8B 模型上平均提升了 6.2% 的准确率，覆盖域内与域外任务，且仅需在 8 张 H800 GPU 上训练 28 分钟。代码地址：https://github.com/Visual-AI/SPoT

title: Inference-Time Safety For Code LLMs Via Retrieval-Augmented Revision
authors: Manisha Mukherjee, Vincent J. Hellendoorn
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed for code generation in high-stakes software development, yet their limited transparency in security reasoning and brittleness to evolving vulnerability patterns raise critical trustworthiness concerns. Models trained on static datasets cannot readily adapt to newly discovered vulnerabilities or changing security standards without retraining, leading to the repeated generation of unsafe code. We present a principled approach to trustworthy code generation by design that operates as an inference-time safety mechanism. Our approach employs retrieval-augmented generation to surface relevant security risks in generated code and retrieve related security discussions from a curated Stack Overflow knowledge base, which are then used to guide an LLM during code revision. This design emphasizes three aspects relevant to trustworthiness: (1) interpretability, through transparent safety interventions grounded in expert community explanations; (2) robustness, by allowing adaptation to evolving security practices without model retraining; and (3) safety alignment, through real-time intervention before unsafe code reaches deployment. Across real-world and benchmark datasets, our approach improves the security of LLM-generated code compared to prompting alone, while introducing no new vulnerabilities as measured by static analysis. These results suggest that principled, retrieval-augmented inference-time interventions can serve as a complementary mechanism for improving the safety of LLM-based code generation, and highlight the ongoing value of community knowledge in supporting trustworthy AI deployment.
title_zh: 通过检索增强修订实现代码大模型的推理时安全
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在高风险软件开发中的代码生成应用日益广泛，但其在安全推理方面的透明度有限，且对不断演变的漏洞模式表现出脆弱性，这引发了严重的可信性问题。基于静态数据集训练的模型难以在不重新训练的情况下适应新发现的漏洞或不断变化的安全标准，导致反复生成不安全的代码。本文提出一种基于设计的可信代码生成原则性方法，该方法作为推理阶段的安全机制运行。我们的方法采用检索增强生成技术，识别生成代码中的相关安全风险，并从一个精心筛选的 Stack Overflow 知识库中检索相关的安全讨论，这些讨论随后用于指导 LLM 在代码修订过程中的决策。该设计强调了可信性相关的三个关键方面：（1）可解释性，通过基于专家社区解释的透明安全干预实现；（2）鲁棒性，无需重新训练即可适应不断变化的安全实践；（3）安全对齐，通过在不安全代码进入部署前进行实时干预来保障安全。在真实世界和基准数据集上的实验表明，与仅使用提示（prompting）的方法相比，我们的方法显著提升了 LLM 生成代码的安全性，且静态分析显示未引入新的漏洞。结果表明，基于原则、检索增强的推理阶段干预可作为提升 LLM 代码生成安全性的补充机制，同时也凸显了社区知识在支持可信人工智能部署中的持续价值。

title: GraphScout: Empowering Large Language Models with Intrinsic Exploration Ability for Agentic Graph Reasoning
authors: Yuchen Ying, Weiqi Jiang, Tongya Zheng, Yu Wang, Shunyu Liu, Kaixuan Chen, Mingli Song
abstract: Knowledge graphs provide structured and reliable information for many real-world applications, motivating increasing interest in combining large language models (LLMs) with graph-based retrieval to improve factual grounding. Recent Graph-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (GraphRAG) methods therefore introduce iterative interaction between LLMs and knowledge graphs to enhance reasoning capability. However, existing approaches typically depend on manually designed guidance and interact with knowledge graphs through a limited set of predefined tools, which substantially constrains graph exploration. To address these limitations, we propose GraphScout, a training-centric agentic graph reasoning framework equipped with more flexible graph exploration tools. GraphScout enables models to autonomously interact with knowledge graphs to synthesize structured training data which are then used to post-train LLMs, thereby internalizing agentic graph reasoning ability without laborious manual annotation or task curation. Extensive experiments across five knowledge-graph domains show that a small model (e.g., Qwen3-4B) augmented with GraphScout outperforms baseline methods built on leading LLMs (e.g., Qwen-Max) by an average of 16.7\% while requiring significantly fewer inference tokens. Moreover, GraphScout exhibits robust cross-domain transfer performance. Our code will be made publicly available~\footnote{https://github.com/Ying-Yuchen/_GraphScout_}.
title_zh: GraphScout：赋予大型语言模型内在探索能力，实现智能体图推理
abstract_zh: 知识图谱为众多现实世界应用提供了结构化且可靠的信息，推动了将大型语言模型（LLMs）与基于图的检索相结合以提升事实性基础的日益增长的兴趣。因此，近期的基于图的检索增强生成（GraphRAG）方法引入了语言模型与知识图谱之间的迭代交互，以增强推理能力。然而，现有方法通常依赖于人工设计的指导策略，并通过一组预定义的有限工具与知识图谱进行交互，这在很大程度上限制了图谱的探索能力。为解决这些局限性，我们提出了 GraphScout——一种以训练为中心的代理式图推理框架，配备了更灵活的图谱探索工具。GraphScout 使模型能够自主地与知识图谱交互，生成结构化的训练数据，随后用于对 LLM 进行后训练，从而在无需繁琐的人工标注或任务设计的情况下，将代理式图推理能力内化到模型中。在五个知识图谱领域的大量实验表明，使用 GraphScout 增强的小型模型（如 Qwen3-4B）在平均性能上比基于领先 LLM（如 Qwen-Max）的基线方法高出 16.7%，同时显著减少了推理所需的 token 数量。此外，GraphScout 展现出强大的跨领域迁移能力。我们的代码将公开发布~\footnote{https://github.com/Ying-Yuchen/_GraphScout_}。

title: MIST-RL: Mutation-based Incremental Suite Testing via Reinforcement Learning
authors: Sicheng Zhu, Jiajun Wang, Jiawei Ai, Xin Li
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) often fail to generate correct code on the first attempt, which requires using generated unit tests as verifiers to validate the solutions. Despite the success of recent verification methods, they remain constrained by a "scaling-by-quantity" paradigm. This brute-force approach suffers from a critical limitation: it yields diminishing returns in fault detection while causing severe test redundancy. To address this, we propose MIST-RL (Mutation-based Incremental Suite Testing via Reinforcement Learning), a framework that shifts the focus to "scaling-by-utility". We formulate test generation as a sequential decision process optimized via Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO). Specifically, we introduce a novel incremental mutation reward combined with dynamic penalties, which incentivizes the model to discover new faults while it suppresses functionally equivalent assertions. Experiments on HumanEval+ and MBPP+ demonstrate that MIST-RL outperforms state-of-the-art baselines. It achieves a +28.5% higher mutation score while reducing the number of test cases by 19.3%. Furthermore, we show that these compact, high-utility tests serve as superior verifiers, which improves downstream code reranking accuracy on HumanEval+ by 3.05% over the SOTA baseline with 10 candidate samples. The source code and data are provided in the supplementary material.
title_zh: MIST-RL：基于强化学习的变异增量测试套件生成
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在首次生成代码时常常无法产出正确结果，因此需要借助生成的单元测试作为验证器来检验解决方案的正确性。尽管近期的验证方法已取得一定成功，但它们仍受限于“数量驱动”的扩展范式。这种粗暴的策略存在一个关键缺陷：在故障检测方面收益递减，同时导致严重的测试冗余。为解决这一问题，我们提出 MIST-RL（基于变异的增量测试套件生成强化学习框架），将关注点从“数量扩展”转向“效用扩展”。我们将测试生成建模为一个通过分组相对策略优化（GRPO）进行优化的序列决策过程。具体而言，我们引入了一种新颖的增量变异奖励机制，并结合动态惩罚机制，激励模型发现新的缺陷，同时抑制功能等价的断言。在 HumanEval+ 和 MBPP+ 数据集上的实验表明，MIST-RL 显著优于当前最先进的基线方法。其在保持测试用例数量减少 19.3% 的同时，将变异得分提升了 28.5%。此外，我们还证明，这些紧凑且高价值的测试用例作为验证器时表现更优，在 HumanEval+ 上将下游代码重排序的准确率提升了 3.05%（相较于 SOTA 基线，使用 10 个候选样本）。相关源代码与数据已提供于补充材料中。

title: Words & Weights: Streamlining Multi-Turn Interactions via Co-Adaptation
authors: Chenxing Wei, Hong Wang, Ying He, Zhongxiang Dai, Bo Jiang, F. Richard Yu, Yao Shu
abstract: Test-time policy adaptation for multi-turn interactions (T2PAM) is essential for aligning Large Language Models (LLMs) with dynamic user needs during inference time. However, existing paradigms commonly treat test-time adaptation as a single-axis problem, either purely refining instructions (Prompt Engineering) or only adjusting weights (Test-Time Training), ignoring that interaction failures stem from a coupled mix of ambiguity and incapacity. We argue that these two optimization paths are not merely additive but synergistic: semantic clarity acts as a pre-conditioner for effective parameter updates. To this end, we propose ROSA2, a framework that reformulates interaction as a joint optimization problem over the heterogeneous space of Words and Weights. By mathematically decomposing the error signal, ROSA2 utilizes textual gradients to rectify intent ambiguity and parameter updates to bridge capability gaps. Theoretically, we prove that this co-adaptation strictly reduces the required parameter shift for convergence. Empirically, ROSA2 outperforms state-of-the-art baselines by 30% on MATH while reducing interaction turns by 40%, demonstrating that refining the context unlocks the true potential of parameter updates.
title_zh: 词与权重：通过协同适应简化多轮交互
abstract_zh: 测试时策略自适应（T2PAM）对于在推理阶段使大型语言模型（LLMs）与动态用户需求保持一致至关重要。然而，现有范式通常将测试时适应视为单一维度的问题，要么仅专注于优化指令（提示工程），要么仅调整模型权重（测试时训练），忽略了交互失败源于语义模糊与能力不足的耦合混合问题。我们认为，这两种优化路径并非简单的叠加关系，而是具有协同效应：语义清晰性是实现有效参数更新的前提条件。为此，我们提出了ROSA2框架，将交互过程重新建模为在“词语”与“权重”异构空间上的联合优化问题。通过数学分解误差信号，ROSA2利用文本梯度纠正意图模糊，同时通过参数更新弥补能力差距。理论上，我们证明了这种协同适应能够严格减少达到收敛所需的参数调整量。实验结果表明，ROSA2在MATH数据集上相比最先进基线性能提升30%，同时将交互轮次减少40%，充分证明了优化上下文能够释放参数更新的真正潜力。

title: Conformal Policy Control
authors: Drew Prinster, Clara Fannjiang, Ji Won Park, Kyunghyun Cho, Anqi Liu, Suchi Saria, Samuel Stanton
abstract: An agent must try new behaviors to explore and improve. In high-stakes environments, an agent that violates safety constraints may cause harm and must be taken offline, curtailing any future interaction. Imitating old behavior is safe, but excessive conservatism discourages exploration. How much behavior change is too much? We show how to use any safe reference policy as a probabilistic regulator for any optimized but untested policy. Conformal calibration on data from the safe policy determines how aggressively the new policy can act, while provably enforcing the user's declared risk tolerance. Unlike conservative optimization methods, we do not assume the user has identified the correct model class nor tuned any hyperparameters. Unlike previous conformal methods, our theory provides finite-sample guarantees even for non-monotonic bounded constraint functions. Our experiments on applications ranging from natural language question answering to biomolecular engineering show that safe exploration is not only possible from the first moment of deployment, but can also improve performance.
title_zh: 合规策略控制
abstract_zh: 智能体必须尝试新的行为以进行探索和改进。在高风险环境中，违反安全约束的智能体可能造成伤害，必须立即下线，从而中断其后续的所有交互。模仿旧行为虽然安全，但过度保守会抑制探索。那么，行为改变到何种程度才算过度？我们展示了如何利用任何安全的参考策略作为概率调节器，来调控任何经过优化但未经测试的新策略。通过对安全策略生成的数据进行保形校准，可以确定新策略能够采取行动的激进程度，同时严格遵守用户声明的风险容忍度。与保守优化方法不同，我们的方法无需假设用户已识别出正确的模型类别，也无需调参。与以往的保形方法不同，我们的理论即使在非单调有界约束函数的情况下，也能提供有限样本保证。我们在从自然语言问答到生物分子工程等多个应用中的实验表明，从部署的第一时间起，安全探索不仅可行，还能提升性能。

title: GenDB: The Next Generation of Query Processing -- Synthesized, Not Engineered
authors: Jiale Lao, Immanuel Trummer
abstract: Traditional query processing relies on engines that are carefully optimized and engineered by many experts. However, new techniques and user requirements evolve rapidly, and existing systems often cannot keep pace. At the same time, these systems are difficult to extend due to their internal complexity, and developing new systems requires substantial engineering effort and cost. In this paper, we argue that recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) are starting to shape the next generation of query processing systems. We propose using LLMs to synthesize execution code for each incoming query, instead of continuously building, extending, and maintaining complex query processing engines. As a proof of concept, we present GenDB, an LLM-powered agentic system that generates instance-optimized and customized query execution code tailored to specific data, workloads, and hardware resources. We implemented an early prototype of GenDB that uses Claude Code Agent as the underlying component in the multi-agent system, and we evaluate it on OLAP workloads. We use queries from the well-known TPC-H benchmark and also construct a new benchmark designed to reduce potential data leakage from LLM training data. We compare GenDB with state-of-the-art query engines, including DuckDB, Umbra, MonetDB, ClickHouse, and PostgreSQL. GenDB achieves significantly better performance than these systems. Finally, we discuss the current limitations of GenDB and outline future extensions and related research challenges.
title_zh: GenDB：下一代查询处理——合成，而非工程化
abstract_zh: 传统查询处理依赖于由众多专家精心优化和设计的引擎。然而，新技术和用户需求发展迅速，现有系统往往难以跟上步伐。同时，由于系统内部结构复杂，这些系统难以扩展，而开发新的系统又需要巨大的工程投入和成本。在本文中，我们提出，大型语言模型（LLMs）的最新进展正开始塑造下一代查询处理系统。我们主张利用LLMs为每个传入的查询生成执行代码，而非持续构建、扩展和维护复杂的查询处理引擎。作为概念验证，我们提出了GenDB——一个由LLM驱动的智能体系统，能够根据特定的数据、工作负载和硬件资源，生成实例化优化且定制化的查询执行代码。我们实现了一个GenDB的早期原型，该原型以Claude Code Agent作为多智能体系统中的底层组件，并在OLAP工作负载上进行了评估。我们使用广为人知的TPC-H基准测试中的查询，同时构建了一个新基准，旨在减少LLM训练数据中潜在的数据泄露问题。我们将GenDB与当前最先进的查询引擎（包括DuckDB、Umbra、MonetDB、ClickHouse和PostgreSQL）进行了对比，结果表明GenDB在性能上显著优于这些系统。最后，我们讨论了GenDB当前的局限性，并展望了未来可能的扩展方向及相关研究挑战。

title: Bespoke OLAP: Synthesizing Workload-Specific One-size-fits-one Database Engines
authors: Johannes Wehrstein, Timo Eckmann, Matthias Jasny, Carsten Binnig
abstract: Modern OLAP engines are designed to support arbitrary analytical workloads, but this generality incurs structural overhead, including runtime schema interpretation, indirection layers, and abstraction boundaries, even in highly optimized systems. An engine specialized to a fixed workload can eliminate these costs and exploit workload-specific data structures and execution algorithms for substantially higher performance. Historically, constructing such bespoke engines has been economically impractical due to the high manual engineering effort. Recent advances in LLM-based code synthesis challenge this tradeoff by enabling automated system generation. However, naively prompting an LLM to produce a database engine does not yield a correct or efficient design, as effective synthesis requires systematic performance feedback, structured refinement, and careful management of deep architectural interdependencies. We present Bespoke OLAP, a fully autonomous synthesis pipeline for constructing high-performance database engines tightly tailored to a given workload. Our approach integrates iterative performance evaluation and automated validation to guide synthesis from storage to query execution. We demonstrate that Bespoke OLAP can generate a workload-specific engine from scratch within minutes to hours, achieving order-of-magnitude speedups over modern general-purpose systems such as DuckDB.
title_zh: 定制化OLAP：针对工作负载的“量身定制”数据库引擎
abstract_zh: 现代OLAP引擎旨在支持任意的分析工作负载，但这种通用性带来了结构性开销，包括运行时模式解析、间接层以及抽象边界，即使在高度优化的系统中也是如此。而针对固定工作负载专门设计的引擎则可以消除这些开销，并利用工作负载特定的数据结构和执行算法，实现显著更高的性能。历史上，构建此类定制化引擎因需要大量人工工程投入而经济上不可行。然而，基于大语言模型（LLM）的代码生成技术的最新进展，正在挑战这一权衡。尽管如此，简单地向LLM发出生成数据库引擎的指令，并无法得到正确或高效的架构设计，因为有效的代码合成需要系统性的性能反馈、结构化的迭代优化，以及对深层架构依赖关系的精细管理。我们提出了Bespoke OLAP，一个完全自主的合成流水线，能够为特定工作负载构建高性能、高度定制化的数据库引擎。我们的方法通过迭代性能评估与自动化验证，从存储到查询执行的各个环节引导合成过程。实验表明，Bespoke OLAP能够在几分钟到几小时内从零开始生成针对特定工作负载的引擎，其性能相比现代通用系统（如DuckDB）可实现数量级的提升。

title: Reasoning as Gradient: Scaling MLE Agents Beyond Tree Search
authors: Yifei Zhang, Xu Yang, Xiao Yang, Bowen Xian, Qizheng Li, Shikai Fang, Jingyuan Li, Jian Wang, Mingrui Xu, Weiqing Liu, Jiang Bian
abstract: LLM-based agents for machine learning engineering (MLE) predominantly rely on tree search, a form of gradient-free optimization that uses scalar validation scores to rank candidates. As LLM reasoning capabilities improve, exhaustive enumeration becomes increasingly inefficient compared to directed updates, analogous to how accurate gradients enable efficient descent over random search. We introduce \textsc{Gome}, an MLE agent that operationalizes gradient-based optimization. \textsc{Gome} maps structured diagnostic reasoning to gradient computation, success memory to momentum, and multi-trace execution to distributed optimization. Under a closed-world protocol that isolates architectural effects from external knowledge, \textsc{Gome} achieves a state-of-the-art 35.1\% any-medal rate on MLE-Bench with a restricted 12-hour budget on a single V100 GPU. Scaling experiments across 10 models reveal a critical crossover: with weaker models, tree search retains advantages by compensating for unreliable reasoning through exhaustive exploration; as reasoning capability strengthens, gradient-based optimization progressively outperforms, with the gap widening at frontier-tier models. Given the rapid advancement of reasoning-oriented LLMs, this positions gradient-based optimization as an increasingly favorable paradigm. We release our codebase and GPT-5 traces.
title_zh: 作为梯度的推理：将最大似然估计代理扩展至树搜索之外
abstract_zh: 基于大语言模型（LLM）的机器学习工程（MLE）代理主要依赖于树搜索，这是一种无梯度优化方法，通过标量验证分数对候选方案进行排序。随着LLM推理能力的不断提升，与随机搜索相比，穷举枚举的效率日益低下，而有方向性的更新则愈发高效，正如精确梯度能够实现比随机搜索更高效的下降。我们提出 \textsc{Gome}，一种实现基于梯度优化的MLE代理。\textsc{Gome} 将结构化诊断推理映射为梯度计算，将成功记忆转化为动量机制，并通过多轨迹执行实现分布式优化。在封闭世界协议下（该协议将架构效应与外部知识隔离），\textsc{Gome} 在 MLE-Bench 上以单张 V100 GPU 上12小时的受限预算，取得了35.1%的任意奖牌率，达到当前最先进水平。对10个不同模型的扩展实验揭示了一个关键转折点：对于推理能力较弱的模型，树搜索仍具优势，因为它可通过穷举探索弥补不可靠推理的缺陷；而随着模型推理能力的增强，基于梯度的优化逐步超越树搜索，且在前沿水平模型上差距进一步扩大。鉴于推理导向型LLM的快速发展，这表明基于梯度的优化正成为越来越有前景的范式。我们已公开代码库及GPT-5的推理轨迹数据。

title: Compliance as Code: A Study of Linux Distributions and Beyond
authors: Jukka Ruohonen, Esmot Ara Tuli, Hiraku Morita
abstract: Compliance as code is an emerging idea about automating compliance through programmed compliance controls and checks. Given scant existing research thus far, the paper presents an empirical analysis of a compliance as code project addressing open source software (OSS) projects and products. The dataset examined covers a little over 1,500 unique compliance rules designed and implemented for 14 Linux distribution releases from five vendors. According to the results, (1) the coverage of the rules varies across the five vendors. Then, (2) the brief rationales provided for the rules do not exhibit statistical similarities but the short code snippets for these do show similarities to some extent. Furthermore, (3) as many as 24 controls are covered from over 10 different organizations, among them governmental agencies, standardization organizations, and non-profit associations. Finally, (4) the rules can be mapped to the essential cyber security requirements of the Cyber Resilience Act (CRA), although only modest agreement exists among the three authors regarding individual mappings. This observation supports an argument that the compliance as code project studied could be updated with new compliance checks. Given that also operating systems are in the CRA's scope when used in a network-connected product, such an updating would have also practical relevance in the nearby future.
title_zh: 代码合规性：对Linux发行版及其他领域的研究
abstract_zh: “合规即代码”是一种新兴理念，旨在通过编程化的合规控制与检查来实现合规自动化。由于目前相关研究尚不充分，本文对一个“合规即代码”项目进行了实证分析，重点关注开源软件（OSS）项目及产品。研究数据涵盖为五个供应商的14个Linux发行版版本设计和实施的1500多个独特的合规规则。研究结果表明：（1）不同供应商之间的规则覆盖范围存在差异；（2）规则所附简要理由在统计上缺乏相似性，但其对应的简短代码片段在一定程度上表现出相似性；（3）这些规则涉及来自10多个不同组织的24项控制措施，其中包括政府机构、标准化组织以及非营利性协会；（4）这些规则可映射到《网络弹性法案》（CRA）所规定的网络安全基本要求，尽管三位作者在具体映射关系上仅达成有限共识。这一发现支持了如下观点：所研究的“合规即代码”项目可进一步更新以纳入新的合规检查。鉴于操作系统在用于网络连接产品时也属于CRA的监管范围，此类更新在未来不久将具有重要的实际意义。

title: CoVe: Training Interactive Tool-Use Agents via Constraint-Guided Verification
authors: Jinpeng Chen, Cheng Gong, Hanbo Li, Ziru Liu, Zichen Tian, Xinyu Fu, Shi Wu, Chenyang Zhang, Wu Zhang, Suiyun Zhang, Dandan Tu, Rui Liu
abstract: Developing multi-turn interactive tool-use agents is challenging because real-world user needs are often complex and ambiguous, yet agents must execute deterministic actions to satisfy them. To address this gap, we introduce \textbf{CoVe} (\textbf{Co}nstraint-\textbf{Ve}rification), a post-training data synthesis framework designed for training interactive tool-use agents while ensuring both data complexity and correctness. CoVe begins by defining explicit task constraints, which serve a dual role: they guide the generation of complex trajectories and act as deterministic verifiers for assessing trajectory quality. This enables the creation of high-quality training trajectories for supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and the derivation of accurate reward signals for reinforcement learning (RL). Our evaluation on the challenging $τ^2$-bench benchmark demonstrates the effectiveness of the framework. Notably, our compact \textbf{CoVe-4B} model achieves success rates of 43.0\% and 59.4\% in the Airline and Retail domains, respectively; its overall performance significantly outperforms strong baselines of similar scale and remains competitive with models up to $17\times$ its size. These results indicate that CoVe provides an effective and efficient pathway for synthesizing training data for state-of-the-art interactive tool-use agents. To support future research, we open-source our code, trained model, and the full set of 12K high-quality trajectories used for training.
title_zh: CoVe：通过约束引导验证训练交互式工具使用智能体
abstract_zh: 开发多轮交互式工具使用智能体具有挑战性，因为现实世界中的用户需求往往复杂且模糊，而智能体却必须执行确定性动作以满足这些需求。为弥合这一差距，我们提出了 \textbf{CoVe}（\textbf{Co}nstraint-\textbf{Ve}rification），一种后训练阶段的数据合成框架，旨在训练交互式工具使用智能体，同时确保数据的复杂性与正确性。CoVe 首先明确定义任务约束，这些约束具有双重作用：一方面指导生成复杂的执行轨迹，另一方面作为确定性验证器来评估轨迹质量。这一机制使得高质量训练轨迹得以生成，用于监督微调（SFT），并为强化学习（RL）提供精确的奖励信号。我们在具有挑战性的 $τ^2$-bench 基准测试上的评估表明，该框架具有显著有效性。值得注意的是，我们轻量级的 \textbf{CoVe-4B} 模型在航空（Airline）和零售（Retail）领域分别取得了 43.0\% 和 59.4\% 的成功率；其整体性能显著优于同规模的强基线模型，并且在与规模高达其 17 倍的模型对比中仍保持竞争力。这些结果表明，CoVe 为生成顶尖交互式工具使用智能体的训练数据提供了一条高效且有效的路径。为支持未来研究，我们开源了代码、训练好的模型以及用于训练的全部 12,000 条高质量轨迹数据集。

title: AdaPonderLM: Gated Pondering Language Models with Token-Wise Adaptive Depth
authors: Shixiang Song, He Li, Zitong Wang, Boyi Zeng, Feichen Song, Yixuan Wang, Zhiqin John Xu, Ziwei He, Zhouhan Lin
abstract: Test-time scaling via recurrent/iterative Transformers enables large language models to spend more computation at inference, but most pretrained recurrent LMs run a fixed number of iterations, wasting compute on easy tokens and lacking token-wise adaptivity. Following the core idea of Adaptive Computation Time(ACT) and Early Exit(EE), we propose AdaPonderLM, a self-supervised recurrent language model that learns token-wise early exiting during pretraining without manually tuned per-token/per-layer pruning ratios. AdaPonderLM uses iteration-specific MLP gates with a monotonic halting mask to decide when each token stops recurring, and introduces a KV reuse mechanism that reuses cached key/value states for halted tokens, ensuring train--test consistency and practical acceleration. Across Pythia backbones from 70M to 410M (pretraining) and up to 2.8B (continued pretraining), AdaPonderLM reduces inference compute at about 10% while maintaining comparable language modeling perplexity and competitive downstream accuracy. Our analysis shows the learned gates allocate more computation to high-NLL (hard) tokens, exhibiting adaptive computation time behavior in a fully self-supervised setting. Meanwhile, under iso-FLOPs, the learned halting policy consistently outperforms fixed pruning, showing AdaPonderLM allocates compute to the right tokens rather than just reducing average depth.
title_zh: AdaPonderLM：具有逐标记自适应深度的门控思考语言模型
abstract_zh: 通过递归/迭代Transformer实现的测试时缩放，使大型语言模型能够在推理阶段投入更多计算资源，但大多数预训练的递归语言模型都采用固定的迭代次数，导致在简单词元上浪费计算资源，且缺乏针对每个词元的自适应能力。受自适应计算时间（ACT）和提前退出（EE）核心思想的启发，我们提出了AdaPonderLM——一种自监督的递归语言模型，它在预训练阶段学习词元级别的提前退出机制，无需手动设定每个词元或每层的剪枝比例。AdaPonderLM采用与迭代次数相关的MLP门控机制，并结合单调停止掩码，决定每个词元何时停止递归；同时引入键值（KV）状态复用机制，对已停止的词元复用缓存的键值状态，从而保证训练与测试的一致性，并实现实际推理加速。在Pythia系列骨干模型上（从70M到410M，预训练阶段；最高达2.8B，持续预训练阶段），AdaPonderLM在保持与原模型相当的语言建模困惑度和具有竞争力的下游任务准确率的同时，将推理计算量减少了约10%。我们的分析表明，模型学习到的门控机制会为高负对数似然（NLL，即难词元）分配更多计算资源，展现出在完全自监督设置下的自适应计算时间行为。此外，在固定FLOPs条件下，所学习的停止策略始终优于固定剪枝方法，表明AdaPonderLM并非简单降低平均深度，而是将计算资源精准分配给真正需要的词元。

title: Agentic Code Reasoning
authors: Shubham Ugare, Satish Chandra
abstract: Can LLM agents explore codebases and reason about code semantics without executing the code? We study this capability, which we call agentic code reasoning, and introduce semi-formal reasoning: a structured prompting methodology that requires agents to construct explicit premises, trace execution paths, and derive formal conclusions. Unlike unstructured chain-of-thought, semi-formal reasoning acts as a certificate: the agent cannot skip cases or make unsupported claims. We evaluate across three tasks (patch equivalence verification, fault localization, and code question answering) and show that semi-formal reasoning consistently improves accuracy on all of them. For patch equivalence, accuracy improves from 78% to 88% on curated examples and reaches 93% on real-world agent-generated patches, approaching the reliability needed for execution-free RL reward signals. For code question answering on RubberDuckBench Mohammad et al. (2026), semi-formal reasoning achieves 87% accuracy. For fault localization on Defects4J Just et al. (2014), semi-formal reasoning improves Top-5 accuracy by 5 percentage points over standard reasoning. These results demonstrate that structured agentic reasoning enables meaningful semantic code analysis without execution, opening practical applications in RL training pipelines, code review, and static program analysis.
title_zh: 代理代码推理
abstract_zh:
大型语言模型代理能否在不执行代码的情况下探索代码库并推理代码语义？我们研究了这一能力，称之为“代理式代码推理”（agentic code reasoning），并提出了“半形式化推理”（semi-formal reasoning）这一结构化提示方法：要求代理构建明确的前提、追踪执行路径，并推导出形式化结论。与非结构化的思维链（chain-of-thought）不同，半形式化推理如同一份证明证书：代理无法跳过任何情况或做出无依据的断言。我们在三个任务上进行了评估（补丁等价性验证、缺陷定位和代码问答），结果表明，半形式化推理在所有任务中均显著提升了准确率。

在补丁等价性验证任务中，准确率从78%提升至88%（在精心筛选的示例上），并在真实世界中由代理生成的补丁上达到93%，接近实现无需执行即可提供可靠强化学习奖励信号所需的可靠性水平。在RubberDuckBench（Mohammad et al., 2026）的代码问答任务中，半形式化推理取得了87%的准确率。在Defects4J（Just et al., 2014）的缺陷定位任务中，半形式化推理将Top-5准确率相比标准推理方法提升了5个百分点。

这些结果表明，结构化的代理式推理能够实现无需执行代码的有意义的语义代码分析，为强化学习训练流程、代码审查以及静态程序分析等实际应用开辟了新路径。

title: Architecture-Aware Multi-Design Generation for Repository-Level Feature Addition
authors: Mingwei Liu, Zhenxi Chen, Zheng Pei, Zihao Wang, Yanlin Wang, Zibin Zheng
abstract: Implementing new features across an entire codebase presents a formidable challenge for Large Language Models (LLMs). This proactive task requires a deep understanding of the global system architecture to prevent unintended disruptions to legacy functionalities. Conventional pipeline and agentic frameworks often fall short in this area because they suffer from architectural blindness and rely on greedy single-path code generation. To overcome these limitations, we propose RAIM, a multi-design and architecture-aware framework for repository-level feature addition. This framework introduces a localization mechanism that conducts multi-round explorations over a repository-scale code graph to accurately pinpoint dispersed cross-file modification targets. Crucially, RAIM shifts away from linear patching by generating multiple diverse implementation designs. The system then employs a rigorous impact-aware selection process based on static and dynamic analysis to choose the most architecturally sound patch and avoid system regressions. Comprehensive experiments on the NoCode-bench Verified dataset demonstrate that RAIM establishes a new state-of-the-art performance with a 39.47% success rate, achieving a 36.34% relative improvement over the strongest baseline. Furthermore, the approach exhibits robust generalization across various foundation models and empowers open-weight models like DeepSeek-v3.2 to surpass baseline systems powered by leading proprietary models. Detailed ablation studies confirm that the multi-design generation and impact validation modules are critical to effectively managing complex dependencies and reducing code errors. These findings highlight the vital role of structural awareness in automated software evolution.
title_zh: 面向仓库级特性添加的架构感知多设计生成
abstract_zh: 在整个代码库中实现新功能对大型语言模型（LLMs）而言是一项极具挑战性的任务。这一主动式任务要求模型深入理解全局系统架构，以避免对原有功能造成意外破坏。传统的流水线式与代理式框架在此类任务中往往表现不佳，主要原因在于其存在“架构盲视”问题，并依赖于贪婪的单路径代码生成策略。为克服这些局限，我们提出了RAIM——一种面向代码库级功能添加的多设计、架构感知框架。该框架引入了一种定位机制，能够在代码库规模的代码图上进行多轮探索，精准识别跨文件的分散修改目标。尤为重要的是，RAIM摒弃了线性的补丁生成方式，转而生成多种多样且差异化的实现设计方案。随后，系统通过基于静态与动态分析的严格影响感知选择流程，筛选出最符合架构原则的补丁，有效避免系统回归问题。在NoCode-bench Verified数据集上的全面实验表明，RAIM实现了新的最先进性能，成功率达到39.47%，相较于最强基线模型实现了36.34%的相对提升。此外，该方法在多种基础模型上展现出强大的泛化能力，甚至使开源权重模型（如DeepSeek-v3.2）超越依赖领先专有模型的基线系统。详细的消融实验进一步证实，多设计生成与影响验证模块对于有效管理复杂依赖关系、降低代码错误至关重要。这些发现凸显了结构感知在自动化软件演化中的关键作用。

title: What Papers Don't Tell You: Recovering Tacit Knowledge for Automated Paper Reproduction
authors: Lehui Li, Ruining Wang, Haochen Song, Yaoxin Mao, Tong Zhang, Yuyao Wang, Jiayi Fan, Yitong Zhang, Jieping Ye, Chengqi Zhang, Yongshun Gong
abstract: Automated paper reproduction -- generating executable code from academic papers -- is bottlenecked not by information retrieval but by the tacit knowledge that papers inevitably leave implicit. We formalize this challenge as the progressive recovery of three types of tacit knowledge -- relational, somatic, and collective -- and propose \method, a graph-based agent framework with a dedicated mechanism for each: node-level relation-aware aggregation recovers relational knowledge by analyzing implementation-unit-level reuse and adaptation relationships between the target paper and its citation neighbors; execution-feedback refinement recovers somatic knowledge through iterative debugging driven by runtime signals; and graph-level knowledge induction distills collective knowledge from clusters of papers sharing similar implementations. On an extended ReproduceBench spanning 3 domains, 10 tasks, and 40 recent papers, \method{} achieves an average performance gap of 10.04\% against official implementations, improving over the strongest baseline by 24.68\%. The code will be publicly released upon acceptance; the repository link will be provided in the final version.
title_zh: 论文中未提及的内容：为实现论文自动化复现而恢复隐性知识
abstract_zh: 自动化论文复现——从学术论文生成可执行代码——的瓶颈并不在于信息检索，而在于论文不可避免地隐含的隐性知识。我们提出将这一挑战形式化为逐步恢复三类隐性知识：关系性、具身性和集体性知识，并引入\method框架，该框架基于图结构的智能体体系，针对每一类知识设计了专门的机制：节点级关系感知聚合通过分析目标论文与其引用文献之间在实现单元层面的复用与适应关系，恢复关系性知识；执行反馈精炼通过基于运行时信号驱动的迭代调试，恢复具身性知识；图级知识归纳则从具有相似实现方式的论文聚类中提炼集体性知识。在涵盖3个领域、10项任务和40篇近期论文的扩展版ReproduceBench上，\method在平均性能上与官方实现的差距仅为10.04%，相比最强基线提升了24.68%。代码将在论文被接受后公开发布，最终版本将提供仓库链接。

title: Can LLMs Hack Enterprise Networks? -- Replicated Computational Results (RCR) Report
authors: Andreas Happe, Jürgen Cito
abstract: This is the Replicated Computational Results (RCR) Report for the paper ``Can LLMs Hack Enterprise Networks?" The paper empirically investigates the efficacy and effectiveness of different LLMs for penetration-testing enterprise networks, i.e., Microsoft Active Directory Assumed-Breach Simulations. This RCR report describes the artifacts used in the paper, how to create an evaluation setup, and highlights the analysis scripts provided within our prototype.
title_zh: 大语言模型能否入侵企业网络？——复制计算结果（RCR）报告
abstract_zh: 这是论文《大型语言模型能否攻陷企业网络？》的可复现计算结果（RCR）报告。该论文通过实证研究，探讨了不同大型语言模型（LLMs）在渗透测试企业网络方面的有效性与效率，具体场景为微软Active Directory假设被攻破的模拟测试。本RCR报告详细说明了论文中所使用的研究工具，如何搭建评估环境，并重点介绍了我们原型系统中提供的分析脚本。

title: Learning Structured Reasoning via Tractable Trajectory Control
authors: Po-Nien Kung, Zhen Yang, Jeffrey Luo, Cheng-Fu Yang, Haikang Deng, Zi-Yi Dou, Yinfei Yang, Nanyun Peng, Zhe Gan, Kai-Wei Chang
abstract: Large language models can exhibit emergent reasoning behaviors, often manifested as recurring lexical patterns (e.g., "wait," indicating verification). However, complex reasoning trajectories remain sparse in unconstrained sampling, and standard RL often fails to guarantee the acquisition of diverse reasoning behaviors. We propose a systematic discovery and reinforcement of diverse reasoning patterns through structured reasoning, a paradigm that requires targeted exploration of specific reasoning patterns during the RL process. To this end, we propose Ctrl-R, a framework for learning structured reasoning via tractable trajectory control that actively guides the rollout process, incentivizing the exploration of diverse reasoning patterns that are critical for complex problem-solving. The resulting behavior policy enables accurate importance-sampling estimation, supporting unbiased on-policy optimization. We further introduce a power-scaling factor on the importance-sampling weights, allowing the policy to selectively learn from exploratory, out-of-distribution trajectories while maintaining stable optimization. Experiments demonstrate that Ctrl-R enables effective exploration and internalization of previously unattainable reasoning patterns, yielding consistent improvements across language and vision-language models on mathematical reasoning tasks.
title_zh: 通过可处理的轨迹控制进行结构化推理
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型能够展现出涌现式推理行为，通常表现为重复出现的词汇模式（例如“wait”，表示验证）。然而，在无约束采样下，复杂推理路径仍然稀疏，而标准强化学习（RL）往往无法保证获取多样化的推理行为。为此，我们提出一种系统性发现并强化多样化推理模式的方法——结构化推理，该范式在强化学习过程中要求对特定推理模式进行有针对性的探索。为此，我们提出了Ctrl-R框架，通过可处理的轨迹控制来学习结构化推理，主动引导生成过程，激励探索对复杂问题求解至关重要的多样化推理模式。由此产生的行为策略能够实现精确的重要度采样估计，支持无偏的策略内优化。我们进一步在重要度采样权重上引入一个幂次缩放因子，使策略能够选择性地从探索性、分布外的轨迹中学习，同时保持优化过程的稳定性。实验表明，Ctrl-R能够有效实现探索并内化此前难以获得的推理模式，在数学推理任务上显著提升了语言模型和视觉-语言模型的一致性表现。

title: ToolRLA: Fine-Grained Reward Decomposition for Tool-Integrated Reinforcement Learning Alignment in Domain-Specific Agents
authors: Pengbo Liu
abstract: Tool-integrated reasoning agents interleaving natural language deliberation with external API calls show promise for complex multi-step tasks. However, aligning such agents for high-stakes domain-specific deployment is challenging, as existing reinforcement learning uses coarse binary rewards (success/failure) that insufficiently guide nuanced tool invocation in production. We present ToolRLA, a three-stage post-training pipeline (Supervised Fine-Tuning, Group Relative Policy Optimization, Direct Preference Optimization) for domain-specific tool-integrated agents. Its core is a fine-grained reward function with multiplicative correctness decomposition, evaluating tool invocation across four dimensions: format validity, tool selection correctness, invocation efficiency, and domain constraint compliance. Multiplicative composition prioritizes correct tool selection (a prerequisite for meaningful parameter evaluation), while a large negative compliance penalty (λ=10) ensures regulatory adherence. Deployed on a real-world financial advisory copilot (80+ advisors, 1,200+ daily queries, 15+ heterogeneous APIs), ToolRLA achieves 47% higher end-to-end task completion (62% to 91%), 63% lower tool invocation error (38% to 14%), 93% lower regulatory violation (12% to 0.8%), and sub-2-second latency after three months. Ablation studies confirm fine-grained reward decomposition contributes 7 percentage points over coarse additive rewards; generalizability is validated on ToolBench and API-Bank.
title_zh: ToolRLA：面向领域特定智能体的工具集成强化学习对齐中的细粒度奖励分解
abstract_zh: 集成工具的推理代理通过交替进行自然语言推理与外部API调用，在处理复杂多步骤任务方面展现出巨大潜力。然而，在高风险领域特定场景中部署此类代理时，对其对齐（alignment）仍具挑战性，因为现有强化学习方法依赖粗粒度的二元奖励（成功/失败），难以充分指导生产环境中工具调用的细微决策。为此，我们提出ToolRLA——一种三阶段后训练流程（监督微调、组相对策略优化、直接偏好优化），专为领域特定的工具集成代理设计。其核心是一个细粒度奖励函数，采用乘法正确性分解机制，从四个维度评估工具调用质量：格式有效性、工具选择正确性、调用效率以及领域约束合规性。乘法组合方式优先保障工具选择的正确性（这是后续参数评估有意义的前提），同时引入较大的负向合规惩罚（λ=10），确保严格遵守监管要求。在真实世界金融顾问协作系统中部署（覆盖80多位顾问，日均1200+次查询，集成15+种异构API），ToolRLA在三个月后实现端到端任务完成率提升47%（从62%增至91%），工具调用错误率降低63%（从38%降至14%），监管违规率下降93%（从12%降至0.8%），且响应延迟低于2秒。消融实验表明，细粒度奖励分解相比粗粒度加法奖励可带来7个百分点的性能提升；通用性也在ToolBench和API-Bank基准上得到验证。

title: SPACE-Timers -- A Stack-Based Hierarchical Timing System for C++
authors: Geray S. Karademir, Klaus Dolag
abstract: SPACE-Timers are a lightweight hierarchical profiling framework for C++ designed for modern high-performance computing (HPC) applications. It uses a stack-based timing model to capture deeply nested execution patterns with minimal overhead, representing runtime behaviour as a tree of timing nodes with precise attribution. The framework provides structured reports with recursive aggregation, detection of unaccounted time, and compact visual summaries of runtime distribution, supporting both quick inspection and detailed analysis. It also includes checkpointing and error detection mechanisms. SPACE-Timers supports multiple profiling backends, including NVTX, ITT, ROCtx, and Omnitrace, and integrates with the MERIC runtime system to enable energy-aware optimisation. Its successful use in OpenGadget3 demonstrates its effectiveness for large-scale scientific applications.
title_zh: SPACE-Timers — 一种基于堆栈的C++分层定时系统
abstract_zh: SPACE-Timers 是一个专为现代高性能计算（HPC）应用设计的轻量级分层性能分析框架，适用于 C++ 程序。它采用基于栈的计时模型，能够以极低的开销捕获深度嵌套的执行模式，将运行时行为表示为具有精确归属关系的计时节点树。该框架提供结构化报告，支持递归聚合、未计入时间的检测，以及运行时分布的紧凑可视化摘要，既适用于快速检查，也支持深入分析。此外，它还具备检查点（checkpointing）和错误检测机制。SPACE-Timers 支持多种性能分析后端，包括 NVTX、ITT、ROCtx 和 Omnitrace，并与 MERIC 运行时系统集成，实现能耗感知的优化。其在 OpenGadget3 中的成功应用，充分证明了其在大规模科学计算应用中的有效性。

title: Graph-Based Self-Healing Tool Routing for Cost-Efficient LLM Agents
authors: Neeraj Bholani
abstract: Tool-using LLM agents face a reliability-cost tradeoff: routing every decision through the LLM improves correctness but incurs high latency and inference cost, while pre-coded workflow graphs reduce cost but become brittle under unanticipated compound tool failures. We present Self-Healing Router, a fault-tolerant orchestration architecture that treats most agent control-flow decisions as routing rather than reasoning. The system combines (i) parallel health monitors that assign priority scores to runtime conditions such as tool outages and risk signals, and (ii) a cost-weighted tool graph where Dijkstra's algorithm performs deterministic shortest-path routing. When a tool fails mid-execution, its edges are reweighted to infinity and the path is recomputed -- yielding automatic recovery without invoking the LLM. The LLM is reserved exclusively for cases where no feasible path exists, enabling goal demotion or escalation. Prior graph-based tool-use systems (ControlLLM, ToolNet, NaviAgent) focus on tool selection and planning; our contribution is runtime fault tolerance with deterministic recovery and binary observability -- every failure is either a logged reroute or an explicit escalation, never a silent skip. Across 19 scenarios spanning three graph topologies (linear pipeline, dependency DAG, parallel fan-out), Self-Healing Router matches ReAct's correctness while reducing control-plane LLM calls by 93% (9 vs 123 aggregate) and eliminating the silent-failure cases observed in a well-engineered static workflow baseline under compound failures.
title_zh: 基于图的自愈工具路由，实现成本高效的大型语言模型代理
abstract_zh: 使用工具的大型语言模型（LLM）代理面临可靠性与成本之间的权衡：将每个决策都交由LLM处理虽能提升正确性，但会导致高延迟和高昂的推理成本；而预先编码的工作流图虽然降低了成本，但在遭遇未预期的复合工具故障时却变得极为脆弱。我们提出了**自愈路由器**（Self-Healing Router），一种具备容错能力的编排架构，将大多数代理的控制流决策视为路由而非推理。该系统结合了两个核心组件：(i) 并行运行的健康监控器，用于为运行时状态（如工具中断、风险信号等）分配优先级评分；(ii) 带成本权重的工具图，采用Dijkstra算法执行确定性的最短路径路由。当某个工具在执行过程中发生故障时，其相关边的权重被重设为无穷大，系统随即重新计算路径——实现自动恢复，且无需调用LLM。LLM仅在完全无可行路径时才被启用，用于目标降级或升级处理。与以往基于图的工具使用系统（如ControlLLM、ToolNet、NaviAgent）侧重于工具选择与规划不同，我们的贡献在于实现了运行时故障容错、确定性恢复以及二元可观测性——所有故障要么被记录为路径重路由，要么被显式升级，绝不会出现无声跳过的情况。在涵盖三种图结构（线性流水线、依赖有向无环图、并行分叉）的19个场景中，Self-Healing Router在正确性上与ReAct相当，同时将控制平面的LLM调用次数减少了93%（从123次降至9次），并彻底消除了在复杂故障下静态工作流基线中出现的无声失败问题。

title: Bentō: Optimizing Persistent Memory Programs
authors: Sebastião Amaro, João Gonçalves, Miguel Matos
abstract: Persistent Memory (PM) is a new storage technology thatbrings high performance, byte addressability, and persistency for a lesser cost than DRAM. Due to cache volatility and store reordering, developers must use explicit instructions (e.g.: flush and fence) to guarantee that the application state remains consistent upon crashes. This is difficult to get right and, in fact, several tools have been created to detect bugs in PM programs. To overcome this difficulty, programmers tend to be overly conservative, for instance, by enforcing unnecessary ordering constraints, which partially forfeits the performance benefits of using PM. In this paper, we study the impact that different combinations of persistency instructions have in several PM programs and found that a specific combination can lead to performance improvements while preserving the original crash-consistency semantics. Based on these results we developed Bentō an automatic and black-box binary rewriter that can boost the performance of existing PM programs by up to 15% with minimal programmer effort.
title_zh: 便当：优化持久性内存程序
abstract_zh: 持久内存（PM）是一种新型存储技术，其性能高、支持字节寻址，并且具有持久性，而成本却低于DRAM。由于缓存的易失性以及存储重排序问题，开发者必须使用显式指令（如flush和fence）来确保程序在崩溃后状态的一致性。然而，正确使用这些指令非常困难，事实上，已有多种工具被开发出来用于检测PM程序中的错误。为应对这一挑战，程序员往往采取过度保守的策略，例如强制施加不必要的顺序约束，这在一定程度上牺牲了使用PM所带来的性能优势。本文研究了不同持久化指令组合在多个PM程序中的影响，发现特定的指令组合能够在保持原有崩溃一致性语义的前提下，带来性能提升。基于这些发现，我们开发了Bentō——一种自动化的、黑盒的二进制重写工具，仅需极少的程序员投入，即可将现有PM程序的性能提升高达15%。

title: LongRLVR: Long-Context Reinforcement Learning Requires Verifiable Context Rewards
authors: Guanzheng Chen, Michael Qizhe Shieh, Lidong Bing
abstract: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has significantly advanced the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) by optimizing them against factual outcomes. However, this paradigm falters in long-context scenarios, as its reliance on internal parametric knowledge is ill-suited for tasks requiring contextual grounding--the ability to find and reason over externally provided information. We identify a key reason for this failure: a reward based solely on the final answer is too sparse to effectively guide the model for identifying relevant evidence. We formally prove that the outcome-only reward leads to significant vanishing gradients for the context grounding process, rendering learning intractable. To overcome this bottleneck, we introduce LongRLVR to augment the sparse answer reward with a dense and verifiable context reward. This auxiliary signal directly incentivizes the model for selecting the correct grounding information, providing a robust learning gradient that solves the underlying optimization challenge. We validate our method on challenging long-context benchmarks using Qwen and LLaMA models. LongRLVR consistently and significantly outperforms the standard RLVR across all models and benchmarks, e.g., boosting a 14B model's scores on RULER-QA from 73.17 to 88.90 and on LongBench v2 from 39.8 to 46.5. Our work demonstrates that explicitly rewarding the grounding process is a critical and effective strategy for unlocking the full reasoning potential of LLMs in long-context applications. Our code is available at https://github.com/real-absolute-AI/LongRLVR.
title_zh: LongRLVR：长上下文强化学习需要可验证的上下文奖励
abstract_zh: 基于可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）通过针对事实性结果优化，显著提升了大型语言模型（LLMs）的推理能力。然而，该范式在长上下文场景中表现不佳，因其依赖模型内部参数化知识，难以适应需要上下文锚定的任务——即从外部提供的信息中查找并进行推理的能力。我们识别出这一失败的关键原因：仅基于最终答案的奖励信号过于稀疏，无法有效引导模型识别相关证据。我们正式证明，仅依赖结果的奖励会导致上下文锚定过程出现严重的梯度消失问题，从而使学习变得不可行。为克服这一瓶颈，我们提出了LongRLVR，通过引入一种密集且可验证的上下文奖励，补充原有的稀疏答案奖励。这一辅助信号直接激励模型选择正确的锚定信息，提供稳健的学习梯度，从而解决根本的优化难题。我们在Qwen和LLaMA模型上，针对具有挑战性的长上下文基准进行了验证。实验结果表明，LongRLVR在所有模型和基准上均持续且显著优于标准RLVR，例如，将14B模型在RULER-QA上的得分从73.17提升至88.90，在LongBench v2上的得分从39.8提升至46.5。我们的工作表明，显式奖励锚定过程是释放LLMs在长上下文应用中全部推理潜力的关键且有效的策略。代码已开源，地址为：https://github.com/real-absolute-AI/LongRLVR。

title: FT-Dojo: Towards Autonomous LLM Fine-Tuning with Language Agents
authors: Qizheng Li, Yifei Zhang, Xiao Yang, Xu Yang, Zhuo Wang, Weiqing Liu, Jiang Bian
abstract: Fine-tuning large language models for vertical domains remains a labor-intensive and expensive process, requiring domain experts to curate data, configure training, and iteratively diagnose model behavior. Despite growing interest in autonomous machine learning, no prior work has tackled end-to-end LLM fine-tuning with agents. Can LLM-based agents automate this complete process? We frame this as a substantially open problem: agents must navigate an open-ended search space spanning data curation from diverse data sources, processing with complex tools, building a training pipeline, and iteratively refining their approach based on evaluation outcomes in rapidly growing logs--an overall scenario far more intricate than existing benchmarks. To study this question, we introduce FT-Dojo, an interactive environment comprising 13 tasks across 5 domains. We further develop FT-Agent, an autonomous system that mirrors human experts by leveraging evaluation-driven feedback to iteratively diagnose failures and refine fine-tuning strategies. Experiments on FT-Dojo demonstrate that purpose-built fine-tuning agents significantly outperform general-purpose alternatives, with FT-Agent achieving the best performance on 10 out of 13 tasks across all five domains. Ablations show that the approach generalizes effectively to 3B models, with additional insights on data scaling trade-offs and backbone sensitivity. Case analyses reveal that agents can recover from failures through cumulative learning from historical experience, while also exposing fundamental limitations in causal reasoning--highlighting both the promise and current boundaries of autonomous LLM fine-tuning.
title_zh: FT-Dojo：面向语言智能体的自主大模型微调
abstract_zh: 将大型语言模型微调至垂直领域仍然是一个耗时且昂贵的过程，需要领域专家参与数据整理、训练配置以及迭代诊断模型行为。尽管自主机器学习领域日益受到关注，但此前尚无研究尝试通过智能体实现端到端的大型语言模型微调。基于大语言模型的智能体能否自动化完成这一完整流程？我们将其视为一个尚未充分探索的开放性问题：智能体必须在开放式的搜索空间中进行探索，涵盖从多样化数据源中进行数据整理、使用复杂工具进行数据处理、构建训练流水线，并根据不断增长的日志中评估结果迭代优化自身策略——这一整体场景远比现有基准测试复杂得多。为研究该问题，我们提出了FT-Dojo，一个包含5个领域共13个任务的交互式环境。我们进一步开发了FT-Agent，一种自主系统，其通过评估驱动的反馈机制，模仿人类专家，迭代诊断失败并优化微调策略。在FT-Dojo上的实验表明，专为微调任务设计的智能体显著优于通用型替代方案，FT-Agent在全部5个领域中的13个任务里，有10个任务取得了最佳表现。消融实验显示，该方法在3B规模模型上也具有良好的泛化能力，并揭示了数据规模扩展的权衡关系以及模型主干对性能的敏感性。案例分析表明，智能体能够通过从历史经验中累积学习来实现故障恢复，同时也暴露出其在因果推理方面的根本局限，凸显了自主大语言模型微调所蕴含的巨大潜力与当前的技术边界。

title: LFPO: Likelihood-Free Policy Optimization for Masked Diffusion Models
authors: Chenxing Wei, Jiazhen Kang, Hong Wang, Jianqing Zhang, Hao Jiang, Xiaolong Xu, Ningyuan Sun, Ying He, F. Richard Yu, Yao Shu, Bo Jiang
abstract: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has achieved remarkable success in improving autoregressive models, especially in domains requiring correctness like mathematical reasoning and code generation.
